name: 2027-01-15 Constitutional Awareness Sovereignty Quote Corrected Section 1 FB+IG+TH
description:
The 1987 Philippine Constitution opens with one weighty sentence: sovereignty resides in the people and all government authority emanates from them.

Read it again. The mandate is unambiguous. From the people. Not from any office, any party, any dynasty.

38 years on, it sometimes reads more as aspiration than as instruction.

Tama na ang theatrics. The Constitution is still in force.

Read it again. The mandate is unambiguous. From the people. Not from any office, any party, any dynasty.

38 years on, it sometimes reads more as aspiration than as instruction.

Tama na ang theatrics. The Constitution is still in force.

The 1987 Philippine Constitution opens with one weighty sentence: sovereignty resides in the people and all government authority emanates from them.

Read it again. The mandate is unambiguous. From the people. Not from any office, any party, any dynasty.

38 years on, it sometimes reads more as aspiration than as instruction.

Tama na ang theatrics. The Constitution is still in force.

A weathered Philippine flag waving against a dramatic dawn sky over a quiet provincial road. No people in frame. Wide cinematic angle. Decorative civic iconography (scales, columns, abstracted seals) framing the corners with restraint.

{"quote": "Sovereignty resides in the people and all government authority emanates from them.", "attribution": "Article II Section 1 - 1987 Philippine Constitution", "footer": "THE FILIPINO STANDARD"}
